slides.title: Operators in Dart ➕
slides.content:
# Operators

Dart supports many operators:

**Arithmetic:** `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `~/` (integer division), `%` (remainder)

**Comparison:** `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`

**Logical:** `&&` (and), `||` (or), `!` (not)

**Assignment:** `=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.

**Type test:** `is`, `is!`, `as`

quizData.question: What does the `~/` operator do?
quizData.explanation: `~/` performs integer division, returning an integer result (floor).

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.codeSnippet:
void main() {
  int a = 10, b = 3;
  print(a + b);   // 13
  print(a - b);   // 7
  print(a * b);   // 30
  print(a / b);   // 3.333...
  print(a ~/ b);  // 3 (integer division)
  print(a % b);   // 1 (remainder)
  
  print(a > b);   // true
  print(a == b);  // false
  
  bool x = true, y = false;
  print(x && y);  // false
  print(x || y);  // true
}

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use parentheses for clarity

Operator precedence can be tricky. Use `( )` to make expressions clear, e.g., `(a + b) * c` instead of relying on precedence.
